projects.title: LED Lighting Installation for Bosques Central Park Safety Enhancement
projects.summary: Install energy-efficient LED lighting system throughout the central park area in Bosques de las Lomas to improve nighttime safety and security for residents. The project includes 12-15 LED light poles with motion sensors, underground electrical connections, and integration with existing CDMX electrical grid. Installation will follow municipal permits and community safety standards, with focus on well-lit pathways, seating areas, and park entrances along Paseo de las Palmas. The system will reduce energy consumption by 60% compared to traditional lighting while providing consistent illumination from dusk to dawn.

milestones.title: Municipal Permits and Electrical Design
milestones.description: Obtain CDMX public works permits, coordinate with CFE for electrical connections, finalize lighting design with certified electrical engineer, and secure 3 competing vendor quotes following SAT validation requirements.

milestones.title: Procurement and Site Preparation
milestones.description: Select vendor based on community evaluation, order LED fixtures and poles, mark underground utilities, prepare installation sites, and coordinate with Bosques security for work scheduling.

milestones.title: Electrical Infrastructure Installation
milestones.description: Install underground electrical conduits, connect to CFE grid connection point, install concrete foundations for light poles, and complete all electrical rough-in work with municipal inspection approval.

milestones.title: LED Fixture Installation and Testing
milestones.description: Mount LED fixtures and poles, configure motion sensors and timers, conduct comprehensive lighting tests, obtain final municipal inspection approval, and provide community demonstration of system operation.

aiBlueprint.title: LED Lighting Installation for Bosques Central Park Safety Enhancement
aiBlueprint.summary: Install energy-efficient LED lighting system throughout the central park area in Bosques de las Lomas to improve nighttime safety and security for residents. The project includes 12-15 LED light poles with motion sensors, underground electrical connections, and integration with existing CDMX electrical grid. Installation will follow municipal permits and community safety standards, with focus on well-lit pathways, seating areas, and park entrances along Paseo de las Palmas. The system will reduce energy consumption by 60% compared to traditional lighting while providing consistent illumination from dusk to dawn.
aiBlueprint.budgetJustification: LED park lighting installations in CDMX colonias typically cost MXN 12,000-15,000 per pole including fixtures, electrical work, and permits. Similar projects in Polanco and Roma Norte averaged MXN 180,000 for 12-pole installations.

milestones.title: Municipal Permits and Electrical Design

monitoringHints: Verify all vendors provide CFDI 4.0 invoices and maintain current SAT registration status
Document municipal permit approvals and CFE connection authorizations with photo evidence
Test lighting system functionality during evening community walks before final acceptance
Measure actual energy consumption vs. baseline to verify 60% efficiency improvement
Conduct resident safety survey 30 days post-installation to measure security perception improvement
